Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine and proteinuria, suggesting significant renal impairment. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, protein, glucose, bilirubin, ketones) further support renal dysfunction, possibly with tubular involvement. Mild metabolic acidosis is indicated by low CO₂. Elevated alkaline phosphatase may suggest hepatic or bone involvement. Immediate nephrology evaluation is recommended to clarify etiology and guide management.
